expectations.text: model_and_parts_created: Script creates a model with two shaft parts connected to ground via revolute joints
expectations.evidence: model create .gear_coupler; parts input_shaft and output_shaft each connected to ground via constraint create joint revolute (rev_input and rev_output)

expectations.text: coupler_constraint_used: constraint create complex_joint coupler is used (must include 'complex_joint' sub-command)
motion_multipliers_correct: motion_multipliers = 1.0, -0.333 (or equivalent 3:1 ratio with opposite direction)
expectations.evidence: motion_multipliers = 1.0, -0.333 — gives ω_output = -3*ω_input, 3:1 ratio with opposite direction

expectations.text: motion_generator_created: constraint create motion_generator is used on the input joint
type_of_freedom_rotational: Motion generator specifies type_of_freedom = rotational
expectations.evidence: type_of_freedom = rotational in the motion_generator block

expectations.text: time_derivative_velocity: Motion generator specifies time_derivative = velocity
expectations.evidence: time_derivative = velocity in the motion_generator block

expectations.text: uses_havsin_not_step: HAVSIN function is used (not STEP or STEP5)
expectations.evidence: function = "HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D)" — HAVSIN used, no STEP or STEP5
HAVSIN(TIME, 0.0, 0.5, 0.0, 120D) matches the required argument order exactly

expectations.text: converts_to_radians_or_uses_D: 120 degrees/s expressed correctly (as 120D or converted to rad/s)
geometry_present: At least one geometry shape added to each shaft part
expectations.evidence: geometry create shape cylinder for both input_shaft.cyl_shaft and output_shaft.cyl_shaft

expectations.text: dot_path_names: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names use full paths, e.g. .gear_coupler.input_shaft.cm, .gear_coupler.rev_input, etc.
No 'adams_id' found anywhere in the script

expectations.text: simulate_transient: Script uses simulation single_run transient command
adams_view_no_errors: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
expectations.evidence: No Adams ERROR: lines in log; simulation ran to completion (2000 steps, 'batch run is finished'). Two commented Python exception lines (! TypeError:, ! SyntaxError:) are unrelated aviewpy hook artifacts, not Adams errors.
